rulings.Case Title: ELVIA JUAREZ VS. ANTHONY E NOVAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order For Discovery Sanctions Against Anthony E. Novak
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, May 22 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ELVIA JUAREZ's Motion For Order For Discovery Sanctions Against Anthony E. Novak Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part; denied in part. The Court finds that Defendant Anthony Novak has failed to comply with the Court's order of February 13, 2014. Defendant shall provide verified responses to the RFPD's at issue and produce any responsive documents no later than June 2, 2014, and pay sanctions to Plaintiff in the amount of $500 by June 16, 2014. The Court finds that Plaintiff's request for evidentiary sanctions is premature at this juncture, however, any failure to comply with this Order will result in evidentiary or terminating sanctions being imposed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EDUARDO BUSTAMANTE VS. YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alt Summ Adj
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 22, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. The motion for summary judgment is denied. There exists a triable issue of material fact regarding the existence of an employment relationship and/or whether Mr. Tran was the ostensible agent of defendant Yellow Cab Cooperative, Inc. Therefore, there is an issue of fact whether defendant is vicariously liable on the negligence and assault/battery causes of action. The court denies summary adjudication on the negligent, hiring, training, retention, supervision, and instruction cause of action. Defendant never provided any training to Mr. Tran regarding how to handle a fare dispute (Plaintiff's Fact 181) and did nothing to prevent Mr. Tran from carrying a tire iron in the driver's compartment (Plaintiff's fact 182). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CANDLESTICK VIEW HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, VS. J.L. BUILDERS, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 22, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT HMI ENTERPRISES INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend. The entire complaint is barred by res judicata. In this case, Plaintiff and general contractor arbitrated their claims, and the Court confirmed the arbitration award. (See Req. Jud. Notice, Exhibit B.) The doctrine of res judicata applies to arbitration proceedings. (7 Witkin, Cal. Proc. (5th ed. 2008) Judgment, § 362, p. 982.) The arbitrator found that Plaintiff could not establish its damages claims against the general contractor. (Req. Jud. Notice, Exhibit 2, p. 5 - 6.) In the instant action, Plaintiff brings suit against the subcontractors of the general contractor on the same project. (Complaint, 16.) The arbitration provision applies to the subcontractors' work under the same project. (Req. Jud. Notice, Exhibit 2, Art. 1.10, 1.12, 29.1.) Res judicata applies to all matters that were raised or could have been raised. (7 Witkin, Cal. Proc. (5th ed. 2008) Judgment, § 362, p. 982.) " 'A party cannot by negligence or design withhold issues and litigate them in consecutive actions. Hence the rule is that the prior judgment is res judicata on matters which were raised or could have been raised, on matters litigated or litigable.' [Citations.]" (Thibodeau v. Crum (1992) 4 Cal.App.4th 749, 755.) Leave to amend is granted, if Plaintiff can do so in good faith, to allege either successive breaches of obligations, separate and distinct torts, or new rights accrued after arbitration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CONVALESCENT CENTER, MISSION ST., INC. DBA SAN VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC HEALTH
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summ Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 22, 2014, Line 9, PLAINTIFF CONVALESCENT CENTER, MISSION ST., INC. DBA SAN FRANCISCO NURSING CENTER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment is granted on the grounds that the Defendant cannot establish that the violations alleged in Citation No. 22-1163-0007568-F (the "Citation") were a direct, proximate cause of Barbara M.'s death. The Citation lists five grounds for the violation of 42 C.F.R 483.13(b): (1) negligent monitoring, supervision, and evaluation of Maximo Fajardo's hiring, training, and orientation process; (2) failure to complete/document a Post Training Performance Evaluation; (3) failure to develop a policy and procedure on Post Training Performance Evaluation; (4) failure to conduct an orientation and direction to make sure Fajardo understood the needs of his residents; and (5) failure to check on Fajardo because it was bathing day for the residents and another Certified Nursing Assistant was unsure if Fajardo was capable of bathing the patients residents to his care. (Declaration of Jillian M. Somers, Ex. C.) Plaintiff met its initial burden of making a prima facie showing that Defendant cannot provide evidence that the above violations were "a direct proximate cause" of Barbara M.'s death, as required by Cal. Health & Safety Code sec. 1424(c) and 1428(e). (UMF 1-19.) Defendant did not meet its burden of providing evidence establishing that the above violations were a direct proximate cause of Barbara M.'s death. Rather, Defendant argues that there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether additional violations, ie: violations not included in the citation, were a proximate cause of Barbara M.'s death. Defendant cites no authority that permits additional violations to be raised for the first time on appeal of the citation to the Superior Court. Health & Safety Code sec. 1424(b) provides that the Department can raise new facts in support of the amount of the penalty assessed, but it does not provide that new violations may be raised. Additionally, the Court finds that collateral estoppel does not apply because the Statement of Deficiencies was not a final administrative decision. The facts in Murray v. Alaska Airlines, Inc. (2010) 50 Cal.4th 860, relied on by Defendant in support of its collateral estoppel argument, are distinguishable from the facts present here. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A. VS. PHILLIP P WILEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 22, 2014, Line 12, PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A.'s MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. The motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. Defendant filed an amended answer on March 17, 2014, that renders the instant motion moot. Defendant should have obtained leave of court before filing the amended answer. Nevertheless, there is little point in striking the amended answer because the court would have granted the instant motion with leave to amend. The amended answer does not necessarily contradict the original answer and plaintiff fails to show that the amended answer is a sham pleading. rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA SERVICE BUREAU, INC. VS. DAVID V VINER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 22, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANT DAVID VINER's Motion To Dismiss. Continued to June 17, 2014 to give opposing party the opportunity to file its opposition. Department 302 has received a courtesy copy of the opposition, but the opposition was not filed, as the document does not appear on the Court's Register of Actions. Opposing party must file the opposition by May 22, 2014, or the motion will be granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MARINE EXCHANGE OF THE SAN FRANCISCO BAY REGION VS. ROB B. STRAHAN (Provide Access)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Osc Re: Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 22, 2014, Line 16, Osc Re: Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
